subject: [PATCH v3 1/8] io_uring/kbuf: add support for kernel-managed buffer rings

2026-03-06.patch_summary: This patch adds support for kernel-managed buffer rings to the io_uring feature. It allows the kernel to allocate and manage the backing buffers for a buffer ring, eliminating the need for applications to provide and manage them. The patch introduces new flags and structures to enable this functionality, including IOU_PBUF_RING_KERNEL_MANAGED and the buf_size field in the io_uring_buf_reg struct. When an application creates a kernel-managed buffer ring, it must set these flags and use mmap(2) to obtain a virtual mapping of the buffers. This approach simplifies memory management for applications using io_uring and improves performance by reducing overhead associated with user-space buffer allocation.
